title: carmichaelia flagelliformis var. corymbosa
intro: Carmichaelia flagelliformis var. corymbosa, also known as the New Zealand broom, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its compact growth habit and clusters of white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, with reported medicinal uses including treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Carmichaelia flagelliformis var. corymbosa include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the leaves and stems, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures.
